mentions.note: Trade-down retail theme; financially challenged families moving to discount retailers. Buyable on pullback if oil shock hits stocks and rate environment improves.
Down 2.2%; off-price competitor weakening alongside TJX; challenged consumer evident.
9% same-store sales growth; off-price leader outperforming OLLI.
Best numbers of entire retail group yet worst S&P performer—head-scratcher; investor irrationality in war-driven selloff.

Under Jim Conroy (ex-Boot Barns), improved merchandising, marketing, in-store execution driving 17% comps, 20% revenue growth, 27% earnings. Path to 3,600 stores from 2,300. Off-price thesis working despite stretched consumer.
